title: Intensely Romantic Canadian Egg Quiche
description: A low-calorie, flaky, and romantic Canadian dish featuring egg yolks in a tandoor clay pot, perfect for a special occasion. It is a unique and delicious twist on traditional quiche.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and salt.
Add the cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the ice water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Wrap the dough in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
On a lightly floured surface, roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Carefully place the dough into a tandoor clay pot, trimming any excess dough from the edges.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and a pinch of salt.
Add the diced ham, grated cheese, and chopped parsley to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour the egg mixture into the pastry-lined clay pot and smooth the top.
Bake the quiche for 35-40 minutes, or until the edges are golden brown and the center is set.
